subject: Re: Karen

subject: Tyler

Henderson,

Found an Eames lounge and ottoman on Facebook Marketplace — guy in Temecula, asking $2,800, says it's "original 1970s." Photos look decent but the leather reads almost too dark and the shell color is off. Sending the listing link separately since I
subject: Eames lounge — that Marketplace listing

body_preview:
Henderson,

Skip it. That leather is a recoverer's job — someone paid $400 to re-dye a cracked shell and is now asking $2,800 hoping a millennial finds it before a dealer does. The 1970s production runs have a specific label on the base: 5-digit date stamp, Her
subject: Re: Eames lounge — that Marketplace listing

body_preview:
Mom,

Leo wanted me to ask about the label photo — turns out it's a Vitra logo, and you called it in about thirty seconds flat. Still coming Saturday — Karen mentioned you're doing the farmers market in the morning, so we'll meet you both there and head to the showroom after. Tyler also said you
subject: Re: Mom

body_preview:
Tyler,

Tell Leo Vitra means post-1984 at the earliest, almost certainly a 2000s reproduction. Good instinct on his part to ask for the label. Farmers market is Karen's thing. I carry the bag and eat a tamale. Meet us at 9. The Rancho Mirage sale opens at 10 and I want through
subject: Re: Mom

subject: Scott

subject: Re: Ashley

subject: Hi Ashley

body_preview:
Jenna,

Go Friday. The Eames lounge: pass immediately if the shells have stress fractures or the base has been refinished — dealers have flooded the market with 'restored' ones and buyers are wising up. The Noguchi is worth the drive only if it's the IN-5

Walked it this morning. The Eames is a 670/671 — rosewood shells, no cracks, base is original, ottoman leather has one split seam we can address easily. Noguchi is the IN-50, small chip on one corner of the glass, nothing structural. Tulip chairs
subject: Re: Ashley

subject: Re: Ashley —

subject: Re: Megan —
